user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: HSBC AGM: Asia Wealth Tops $1 Trillion — Execution Is Now the Public Scoreboard
talking_points.why_it_matters: Elhedery's shareholder-facing confirmation of $1 trillion+ Asia wealth and upgraded financial targets turns Adam's WPB APAC change programme into the delivery engine for metrics that are now publicly committed.
bullets: Georges Elhedery, Group CEO of HSBC, told shareholders today that bank-wide wealth balances reached $2.1 trillion at end-2025, with more than $1 trillion booked in Asia — the first public milestone confirmation of the Asia wealth thesis.

HSBC upgraded 2026 banking NII guidance to ~$46 billion and locked in RoTE targets of 17%+ for 2026, 2027, and 2028, giving the WPB change programme a three-year performance floor it must operationally support.

Brendan Nelson, Group Chairman of HSBC, confirmed a thorough review of a $400 million fraud-related Q1 provision with risk appetite being updated — indicating that risk and control delivery is now an AGM-level priority, not just a back-office matter.

source_links.title: HSBC AGM: Our Growth Strategy Is Delivering
Bloomberg: HSBC Chair on $400M Fraud Provision

talking_points.headline: FIS-Anthropic AML Agent: Days to Minutes — HSBC's AI Change Teams Now Have a Live Benchmark
talking_points.why_it_matters: The FIS-Anthropic Financial Crimes AI Agent deploys at production scale in H2 2026, giving Adam a live competitor-adopted benchmark that HSBC's AI-enabled change programme must now be measured against.
bullets: FIS's Financial Crimes AI Agent automatically assembles evidence across core banking systems, evaluates AML alerts against known typologies, and surfaces highest-risk cases for investigator review — compressing case timelines from days to minutes at BMO and Amalgamated Bank.

The agent roadmap extends beyond AML to credit decisioning, deposit retention, customer onboarding, and fraud prevention — embedding a full change agenda inside one AI partnership that H2 2026 deployers will have operational.

Anthropic forward-deployed engineers co-designed the agent with FIS and transferred knowledge for FIS to scale agents independently — this partnership construct is directly applicable to how HSBC WPB should structure its own AI vendor engagements.

source_links.title: FIS: Agentic AI for Banking with Anthropic
FinTech Magazine: FIS and Anthropic for AML

talking_points.headline: Singapore's Wealth Machine Breaks All Records — DBS, UOB, Bank of Singapore Widen the APAC Gap
talking_points.why_it_matters: Three Singapore-based competitors simultaneously posted record wealth AUM and fee metrics in Q1 2026, raising the competitive bar that Adam's WPB APAC change programme must help HSBC close.
bullets: Asian Private Banker reports Singapore's big three hit all-time highs in wealth income in Q1 2026, with DBS wealth fees surging 29% and 58% of client AUM now in investment products — execution quality at scale that Adam's teams must enable at HSBC.

UOB reported Q1 wealth AUM of $156 billion alongside record revenues, confirming that even Singapore's third-largest bank is operating at institutional-grade wealth scale — the competitive floor is rising across the board.

Bank of Singapore surpassed $145 billion AUM and is targeting Asia's top-5 private bank ranking within five years, having already exceeded its Hong Kong UHNW growth target ahead of schedule — a direct market-share threat to HSBC Private Bank.

source_links.title: Asian Private Banker: Singapore Big Three Records
Hubbis: Bank of Singapore Top 5 Target

HSBC News.title: HSBC AGM: Asia Wealth Tops $1 Trillion, Elhedery Upgrades NII to $46bn
HSBC News.summary: At today's London AGM CEO Elhedery confirmed bank-wide wealth balances of $2.1 trillion (>$1trn in Asia), upgraded 2026 NII guidance to ~$46bn, and reaffirmed RoTE 17%+ targets through 2028 — publicly locking the Asia wealth growth thesis as a three-year shareholder commitment.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Chair: $400M Fraud Provision Had Thorough Review, Risk Appetite Updated
HSBC News.summary: Chairman Brendan Nelson told AGM shareholders HSBC conducted a thorough review of a $400 million fraud-related provision in Q1 2026 and is updating its risk appetite framework, signalling that risk and control infrastructure is an active board-level priority alongside growth.

AI & Technology.title: FIS + Anthropic Launch Financial Crimes AI Agent: AML Investigations Days to Minutes
AI & Technology.summary: FIS announced its Financial Crimes AI Agent built with Anthropic automatically assembles AML evidence across core banking systems and compresses case investigations from days to minutes; BMO and Amalgamated Bank deploy in H2 2026 with a roadmap spanning credit, onboarding, and fraud prevention.

AI & Technology.title: Citi Arms Wealth Advisors with 4 AI Tools to Cut Busywork
AI & Technology.summary: Citi deployed four AI tools across its wealth division — one client-facing and three advisor-facing — designed to eliminate low-value tasks and shrink the gap between data and personalised advice, complementing the previously announced Citi Sky AI advisor backed by Google DeepMind.

AI & Technology.title: Lloyds: 2026 Is the Year of Agentic AI and a New Era for Finance
AI & Technology.summary: Lloyds Banking Group's insights post frames 2026 as the inflection year where AI agents move from pilots to enterprise production, with leading banks operationalising agents across experiences and processes powered by real-time data, cloud-native platforms, and interoperable core systems.

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS MFO Foundry Hits SGD 1bn AUM, Targets Doubling to SGD 2bn by End-2026
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS Private Bank's Multi-Family Office Foundry VCC surpassed SGD 1 billion AUM just two years after its 2023 launch and targets SGD 2 billion by end-2026, reflecting strong UHNW demand for institutional-grade family office structuring that positions DBS as a formidable competitor to HSBC Private Bank.

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS Wins Euromoney World's Best for Digital Assets 2026
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Euromoney awarded DBS Private Bank 'World's Best for Digital Assets 2026', citing a decade of digital investment enabling UHNW clients to trade billions in crypto-linked instruments — a fourth Euromoney title adding digital-assets leadership to DBS's existing private banking dominance.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Singapore's Big Three Break Wealth Fee and AUM Records in Q1 2026
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Asian Private Banker reports DBS, OCBC's Bank of Singapore, and UOB all hit all-time highs in wealth income and AUM in Q1 2026, with DBS wealth fees surging 29% and 58% of AUM now in investment products — a market-wide acceleration of Singapore's private banking ecosystem.
